---
title: "Peclet Examples"
subtitle: "See what's possible — then copy, run, and adapt."
toc: false
---

Worked, **runnable** examples for the [`peclet`](https://pypi.org/project/peclet/)
simulation suite: GPU-accelerated particle dynamics, CFD, and the spatial-indexing
primitives they build on. Every example is **self-contained** (it builds its own
geometry and sets every parameter inline), executed to produce the figures and
numbers you see, and one click from running in the browser.

## Gallery

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### [Plane Poiseuille — cut-cell IBM is exact →](examples/poiseuille-ibm/index.qmd)
Body-force channel flow past immersed no-slip walls, reproduced to machine
precision — and the validation trap that makes it *look* inexact.

*flow · cut-cell IBM · SDF · verification*
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### [Pipe flow — second-order convergence →](examples/pipe-poiseuille/index.qmd)
The same parabolic flow through a *curved* cylindrical wall, where the cut-cell
IBM finally shows its genuine O(h²) accuracy — the convergence study the plane
channel can't give.

*flow · cut-cell IBM · SDF · convergence*
:::

:::

*(More examples are landing here through the night: cylindrical pipe flow with
genuine second-order convergence, Zick–Homsy Stokes drag through sphere arrays,
flow past a cylinder and its vortex street, the lid-driven cavity vs Ghia, the
Taylor–Green vortex, packed beds built with `peclet.dem`, and more.)*

::: {.callout-note}
## How these run
Each example installs `peclet` from PyPI in its first cell, so it runs on a free
Colab CPU runtime out of the box. Solver-backed outputs are also **frozen** into
this site, so the pages always build without a GPU.
:::
